resources.string: Slack is locked until %1$s.\n\nEnforcement is on. Opening Slack returns you to the home screen.
Slack Lock runs until %1$s, but enforcement is off.\n\nRe-enable Accessibility to block Slack.
Tap when you are ready to lock Slack until the next 6 AM.
Setup needed: the next tap explains Accessibility access, then opens Android Settings.
No in-app undo. To stop early, disable Slack Lock in Accessibility Settings or uninstall it.
The timer is still active, but Slack is currently reachable because Accessibility is off.

Slack Lock needs Accessibility permission to detect when Slack opens during an active lock and send you back home. 

In the next screen, find "Slack Lock" and toggle it on. 

If the toggle is greyed out (Android 13+), go back to App info, tap the ⋮ menu in the top-right and choose "Allow restricted settings" first.

Slack Lock uses Android Accessibility only to detect when the Slack app opens during an active lock. It receives Slack window-change events and sends you to the home screen. It does not read screen content, messages, typed text, notifications, files, or contacts, and it does not collect or share data.
Opening Slack before %1$s will return you to the home screen. There is no in-app undo. To stop early, disable Slack Lock in Accessibility Settings or uninstall it.
Detects when Slack is opened during an active lock and sends you back to the home screen. Does not collect, store, or transmit any data.
